verdict.answer_first: A gives the plan immediately ('I'll give you the plan directly with sensible defaults'), while B starts by asking me to make decisions ('The three questions that pick your path').
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to a default path and recommends it ('If you can possibly negotiate even a few minutes of read-only time, take the simple path above — it's far less risky'), while B presents options as a choice ('Phase 4 — Cutover (pick by downtime budget)').
verdict.actionability: A's simple 7-step numbered list is a direct checklist. B's phased approach with a branching step requires me to assemble my own plan from its parts, which is slower.
